script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'review-entry-crud-lightbox',
  description: 'Adversarially review the entry backend-CRUD + Telegram-lightbox diff',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Review' },
    { title: 'Verify' },
  ],
}

const CONTEXT = `
Repo: C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/mandarin_reys_hisobot — Telegram Mini App: FastAPI backend (app/db.py, app/server.py) + vanilla-JS frontend (webapp/js/app.js, webapp/index.html, webapp/css/styles.css). Run "git diff" to see the change under review.

The diff adds:
BACKEND
- db.py: add_reys/adjust now return entry_id (lastrowid). New: _get_entry, _apply_balances (all-or-nothing per-type deltas, raises InsufficientStock if any balance would drop below -1e-9), edit_reys (reverse old net, apply new), edit_adjust (reverse old transfer, apply new), delete_entry (undo effect + delete row), ActivityNotFound.
- server.py: PUT /api/report/{entry_id}, PUT /api/adjust/{entry_id} (multipart, same validation as the POSTs), DELETE /api/entry/{entry_id}?report_id= (auth _auth_or_403 state_changing, rate-limited). 404 ActivityNotFound, 409 InsufficientStock.
FRONTEND
- Entries lists per section: {top, topchiqgan, bizda, chiqgan, reys, adjust}; reys/adjust saves push {id: json.entry_id, ..., files, ts}.
- "Yuklanganlar" buttons on both work-area tabs (reysViewBtn/adjViewBtn) → shared paginated viewer keyed by new viewerSection var (was state.formSection).
- Kebab edit: startEditReys (loads type/coef via setCoefUI/weight/photos into tab 1; editingReys; save PUTs), startEditAdjust (tab 2; editingAdj; save PUTs; adjSaveBtn shows "Yangilash: from → to"). deleteEntry is now async: for reys/adjust it DELETEs /api/entry first, updates balances, then splices locally.
- onSave/onAdjustSave: editing branches (snapshot const editing = editingReys/editingAdj before await; Object.assign entry on success; setBusy shows Yangilash while editingReys).
- Lightbox rework: lb state {mode:'form'|'entry', pk?, files?, caption?, i}; lbShow/lbNav; prev/next buttons; counter; caption (entryCaption from entry fields); swipe (touchstart/touchend dx>48); ArrowLeft/ArrowRight keyboard (skipped when focus in INPUT/TEXTAREA; also pages the entries viewer); deleteFromLightbox only in form mode (removes current, clamps index, closes when empty); entry mode uses one ephemeral object URL (lbTempUrl) revoked on switch/close.

Focus ONLY on the changed behavior. Read the files (and git diff) yourself. The Obshiy sections being session-memory-only is BY DESIGN. Backend already passes a 15-case TestClient suite (create/edit/delete/409 guards/404s/cross-report scoping) — look for what those tests DON'T cover.
`;

const DIMENSIONS = [
  { key: 'backend-consistency', prompt: 'Hunt for backend correctness bugs in db.edit_reys/edit_adjust/delete_entry/_apply_balances and the new endpoints: float accumulation vs the -1e-9 epsilon; same-type collisions in edit_adjust deltas (e.g. new from == old to); editing an adjust so from_type==old from; concurrent-lock assumptions; activity row fields left stale (ts/actor); DELETE /api/entry auth path (header initData vs cookie origin) vs how the frontend calls it; rate-limit key choice; report_id type coercion; whether PUT validation diverges from POST anywhere that matters.' },
  { key: 'frontend-state', prompt: 'Hunt for frontend state-machine bugs: editingReys/editingAdj lifecycle (failed PUT leaves what state? navigating away mid-edit? deleting the entry being edited? switching tabs mid-edit? starting an Obshiy edit while a reys edit is pending?); viewerSection vs state.formSection divergence (openEntries from topViewBtn vs reysViewBtn; kebab edit/delete routed by viewerSection after the viewer was reopened for a different section); entry object identity after Object.assign; badge counts; setCoefUI chip restoration for custom and unmatched fixed values; remember-mode interaction with resetForm after edit.' },
  { key: 'lightbox-nav', prompt: 'Hunt for lightbox bugs: lb index vs live array mutation (deleteFromLightbox while grid re-renders; photos added while lightbox open); ephemeral URL leaks or use-after-revoke (lbTempUrl on rapid nav; entryUrls vs lbShow); swipe handler conflicts with existing pinch/drag handlers or scrolling; ArrowLeft/Right hijack cases (focus in a button? select sheet open? entries pager while lightbox open takes precedence — correct?); counter/arrow states with 0 or 1 items; viewEntryPhotos on an entry whose files were replaced by a concurrent edit; closing via backdrop click hitting nav buttons.' },
]

phase('Review')
const results = await pipeline(
  DIMENSIONS,
  (d) => agent(`${CONTEXT}\n\nYou are a skeptical reviewer. ${d.prompt}\n\nReport concrete, real defects only (file:line, failing scenario reachable through real UI/API paths, and why). If nothing solid, return an empty list.`,
    { label: `review:${d.key}`, phase: 'Review', schema: {
      type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
      required: ['findings'],
      properties: { findings: { type: 'array', items: {
        type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
        required: ['summary', 'file', 'line', 'scenario'],
        properties: {
          summary: { type: 'string' },
          file: { type: 'string' },
          line: { type: 'integer' },
          scenario: { type: 'string' },
        } } } } } }),
  (review, d) => parallel((review.findings || []).map((f) => () =>
    agent(`${CONTEXT}\n\nAnother reviewer claims this defect in the CHANGED code:\nSummary: ${f.summary}\nLocation: ${f.file}:${f.line}\nScenario: ${f.scenario}\n\nRead the actual code and try to REFUTE it. Real only if reachable through the app's real navigation/handlers or real API calls (not synthetic). Default to real=false if uncertain or guarded elsewhere.`,
      { label: `verify:${d.key}`, phase: 'Verify', schema: {
        type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
        required: ['real', 'verdict'],
        properties: {
          real: { type: 'boolean' },
          verdict: { type: 'string' },
        } } })
      .then((v) => ({ ...f, ...v }))
  )),
)

const confirmed = results.flat().filter(Boolean).filter((f) => f.real)
return { totalRaw: results.flat().filter(Boolean).length, confirmed }

confirmed.summary: The oversized-body rejection middleware `_limit_body` only checks `request.method == "POST"`, so the new multipart endpoints PUT /api/report/{entry_id} and PUT /api/adjust/{entry_id} completely bypass the MAX_BODY_BYTES (62 MB) cap. FastAPI resolves the Form/UploadFile parameters by parsing the entire multipart body (spooling file parts to temp files) BEFORE the handler runs, i.e. before `_rate_ok` and `_auth_or_403` execute, so the cap the middleware comment promises ("reject oversized request bodies BEFORE FastAPI buffers/spools them") does not apply to PUT at all; the handler-level `_read_capped` 413s only fire after the whole body has already been ingested.
confirmed.scenario: An unauthenticated client sends `PUT /api/report/1` (or /api/adjust/1) with a multi-gigabyte multipart body (e.g. curl -X PUT -F 'photos=@huge.bin'). The server streams and spools the entire body to disk temp files before any auth/rate-limit code runs, then returns 413/403 — repeated or slow-streamed requests exhaust disk/bandwidth. The identical body sent as POST is rejected up front with 413 by the middleware; the PUT variant added in this diff is unprotected. Fix: extend the method check to POST/PUT (any body-bearing method).
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. The `_limit_body` middleware (app/server.py:62) checks only `request.method == "POST"`, so the diff's new multipart endpoints PUT /api/report/{entry_id} and PUT /api/adjust/{entry_id} bypass the MAX_BODY_BYTES (62MB) pre-parse cap entirely. Verified in installed FastAPI 0.136.1 that `get_request_handler` runs `await request.form()` before the endpoint executes — and auth itself consumes a form field (`init_data = Form("")`) — so the entire multipart body is parsed before `_rate_ok` and `_auth_or_403` run. Starlette 0.52 imposes no total/file-part size limit (file parts spool unbounded to disk temp files via SpooledTemporaryFile; max_part_size=1MB applies only to non-file fields; max_files caps count not bytes), and uvicorn configures no body limit. An unauthenticated `PUT /api/report/1` with a huge file part is therefore fully ingested and disk-spooled before returning 403 (or 413 from the post-ingestion _read_capped), while the identical POST is rejected up front with 413 — breaking the invariant the middleware's own comment promises. Only mitigation found is deployment-specific (Cloudflare tunnel's ~100MB edge cap), which still exceeds the 62MB limit and is not a code guard. Fix: extend the middleware check to all body-bearing methods (POST and PUT).

confirmed.summary: In-flight edit collision: the success paths of onSave/onAdjustSave unconditionally clear the editing state (`editingReys = null` at ~line 1747, `editingAdj = null` at ~line 1560) and call resetForm()/resetAdjust(), but nothing prevents the user from starting a second edit while a PUT is still in flight — only the save button is disabled; the "Yuklanganlar" viewer, kebab menu and startEditReys/startEditAdjust remain fully usable (they have no `saving`/`adjusting` guard).
confirmed.scenario: Edit reys entry A that carries several large photos (every save re-uploads all photos, so the PUT takes seconds on mobile). While "Saqlanmoqda…" is showing, open Yuklanganlar → kebab → Tahrirlash on entry B: startEditReys(B) loads B into the form and sets editingReys = B. When A's PUT then resolves, the success branch nulls editingReys and resetForm() wipes B's just-loaded values/photos (revoking their object URLs); setBusy(false) relabels the button "Saqlash". The user re-enters their changes and taps save → the request goes out as POST /api/report, creating a duplicate new entry on the backend instead of PUT-updating entry B (B's intended edit is silently lost). Same flow applies to adjust edits.
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. The second-edit path is fully reachable while a save is in flight: only els.saveBtn is disabled by setBusy(true) (webapp/js/app.js:1793-1796); reysViewBtn -> openEntries("reys") (1181), kebab -> entryEditBtn -> startEditEntry -> startEditReys (1197, 1018, 1057) have no saving/adjusting guard, and body.locked is just overflow:hidden. startEditReys(B) sets editingReys = B and loads B into the form mid-flight. When A's PUT resolves, onSave's success branch runs Object.assign on the snapshot (correct for A) but then UNCONDITIONALLY clears editingReys = null (1772) and calls resetForm() (1782), wiping B's just-loaded values/photos (revoking their object URLs); setBusy(false) relabels the button "Saqlash". The user's re-entered save goes out as POST /api/report, creating a duplicate entry while B stays unedited — silent data loss plus inventory double-count. Identical hole in onAdjustSave (snapshot 1566, unconditional editingAdj = null at 1585, resetAdjust() 1592, unguarded adjViewBtn 1182). deleteEntry (1110-1111) uses the correct conditional pattern (if (editingReys === entry)), which the save success paths omit. Claimed line numbers are slightly off (1772/1585, not ~1747/~1560) but the defect is real as described.

confirmed.summary: Password minimum-length check was relaxed from 8 to 4 characters but the error message still says "password must be at least 8 characters", so the CLI now prints a wrong requirement when rejecting a too-short password.
confirmed.scenario: Run `python -m app.passwords someuser` and enter a 3-character PIN: the tool rejects it with "password must be at least 8 characters" even though a 4-character PIN (the intended new minimum) is accepted — the admin is misled into choosing an 8+ char password or believing 4-digit PINs are unsupported.
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. The working-tree diff changed the check in app/passwords.py _main() from `len(pw) < 8` to `len(pw) < 4` (line 48) but left the error message on line 49 as "password must be at least 8 characters". Reachable via the documented real entry point `python -m app.passwords <user>`: a 3-char password is rejected with the stale "8 characters" message while a 4-char PIN is accepted; no other layer validates length or corrects the message. Minor severity (misleading CLI error text only, no functional/security impact), but the claim is factually accurate and introduced by this change.

confirmed.summary: onSave's success handler unconditionally clears editingReys and calls resetForm(), clobbering an edit session started while the request was in flight — can silently create a duplicate reys entry (double-counted inventory) or wipe/corrupt the newly loaded edit form.
confirmed.scenario: User taps 'Yangilash' to PUT entry X (all photos are re-uploaded, so the request takes seconds on mobile; only saveBtn is disabled). While it is in flight they tap 'Yuklanganlar' (reysViewBtn is not disabled), kebab-edit entry Y — startEditReys(Y) sets editingReys=Y and fills the form. When X's PUT resolves, line 1747 sets editingReys=null and line 1757 resetForm() wipes Y's data; the button flips to 'Saqlash'. The user re-enters Y's values and saves → POST /api/report creates a new entry duplicating Y, adding Y's net to inventory a second time. Variant: if the in-flight request was a create, resetForm() blanks the form while editingReys stays Y (button says 'Yangilash' over an empty form; with remember off the type resets to 'akb'), so refilling and saving PUTs wrong/default values over entry Y. Fix: only clear/reset when editingReys === editing (the snapshot taken at line 1734).
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. Real race in onSave's success handler (webapp/js/app.js). The snapshot `const editing = editingReys` (line 1734) is taken before the multipart fetch (all photos re-uploaded, so it is slow), but the success path unconditionally runs `editingReys = null` (line 1747) and `resetForm()` (line 1757) without checking `editingReys === editing`. During the in-flight PUT only saveBtn is disabled (setBusy, line 1769); reysViewBtn (line 1156) and the kebab edit path (line 1172 -> startEditEntry -> startEditReys, lines 1032-1045) have no `saving` guard, so the user can start editing entry Y mid-flight (editingReys = Y, form filled with Y). When X's PUT resolves, Y's edit session is cleared and Y's form data wiped; the button flips to "Saqlash", so re-entering Y's values POSTs a new entry duplicating Y (inventory double-count). The create-in-flight variant also holds: resetForm() blanks the form while editingReys stays Y, leaving "Yangilash" over an empty form; saving then PUTs wrong/default values over entry Y. No guard exists elsewhere (deleteEntry's identity check at line 1085 shows the pattern the handler lacks; onAdjustSave has the same flaw). Purely client-side, so the backend TestClient suite cannot cover it. Suggested fix (clear/reset only when editingReys === editing) is correct.

confirmed.summary: onAdjustSave has the identical mid-flight clobber: success handler unconditionally sets editingAdj=null and calls resetAdjust(), so an adjust edit started during a slow request is killed and a retry double-moves stock.
confirmed.scenario: User taps 'Yangilash: A → B' to PUT adjust entry X (photos re-uploaded; only adjSaveBtn disabled). While in flight they open 'Yuklanganlar' on tab 2 and kebab-edit adjust entry Y (editingAdj=Y, form filled). X's response runs line 1560 editingAdj=null and line 1567 resetAdjust() (clearing Y's from/to/weight/photos; renderAdjust relabels the button 'Saqlash'). Re-entering Y's transfer and saving POSTs a brand-new transfer instead of updating Y — the weight is moved from from_type twice (or 409s if the balance is now insufficient, leaving the user unable to tell what happened). Same fix as onSave: guard with editingAdj === editing (snapshot at line 1541).
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. onAdjustSave snapshots `const editing = editingAdj` (app.js:1541) but its success handler unconditionally runs `editingAdj = null` (line 1560) and `resetAdjust()` (line 1567) with no `editingAdj === editing` identity check. Only adjSaveBtn is disabled during the multipart PUT (line 1538); adjViewBtn → kebab → edit (startEditAdjust, line 1049) remains fully reachable mid-flight and sets editingAdj to a different entry Y. When X's slow PUT resolves, Y's edit session is silently killed and the form cleared; with "remember" on, the button even shows "Y.from → Y.to" so the user plausibly re-enters the weight and saves — which now POSTs a brand-new transfer (line 1552, editingAdj is null) while Y's original transfer persists on the backend, debiting from_type twice or producing an opaque 409. deleteEntry (line 1086) already uses the identity-check pattern the success handler lacks, confirming the intended guard. Client-side race, not covered by the backend test suite.

confirmed.summary: reys/adjust edit mode has no cancel/exit path and survives navigation away from the work area — the next 'save' silently overwrites the stale entry instead of creating a new one; the parallel Obshiy form explicitly drops its edit on back (formBack), so the asymmetry is unintentional.
confirmed.scenario: User kebab-edits reys entry X (startEditReys line 1032: editingReys=X, form filled, button 'Yangilash'), gets interrupted, presses Telegram Back (onBack line 359: work area → showMenu — nothing clears editingReys, unlike formBack at line 864 which nulls editingEntry on the same gesture). Later they re-enter via 'Kargolarga tarqatish' to record a new trip: the form still carries X; changing the photos/weight/type and saving PUTs over X — X's activity row is rewritten and its original net removed from inventory, instead of a new entry being created. There is no UI to return to create mode: only saving the edit, deleting X, or leaving the report resets it, and if the PUT 404s (entry deleted elsewhere) the user is stuck in edit mode permanently. startEditAdjust (line 1049) has the same gap on tab 2.
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. The claim is accurate on every point. startEditReys (app.js:1032) / startEditAdjust (app.js:1049) set editingReys/editingAdj with no cancel path: the flags are cleared only by a successful PUT (line 1747), deleting that exact entry (line 1085), or clearEntries — which runs only from openReport (line 754). The work-area Back path (onBack line 359 → showMenu) and re-entry (menuDistBtn → openWork line 799) never reset the form or the edit flags, while the parallel Obshiy form's formBack (line 864) explicitly nulls editingEntry on the same Back gesture — confirming the asymmetry. Reachable via real handlers: kebab-edit entry X, press Telegram Back, later re-enter via 'Kargolarga tarqatish' to record a new trip — onSave (line 1734-1737) PUTs to /api/report/X.id, the backend edit_reys reverses X's old net and rewrites its row, and Object.assign overwrites X's local card: the original entry is destroyed and no new entry is created. The 404 sub-claim also holds: on PUT failure editingReys stays set, and deleteEntry returns early when the backend DELETE fails (line 1075-1078), so if the row was deleted elsewhere the only escape is leaving the report. Only mitigation is the 'Yangilash' button text — a hint, not a guard.

confirmed.summary: Password length check was lowered from 8 to 4 but the error message still says 'password must be at least 8 characters'.
confirmed.scenario: Admin runs `python -m app.passwords <user>` and enters a 3-character PIN: the check `len(pw) < 4` (line 48) rejects it but prints 'password must be at least 8 characters', telling the admin the 4-digit PIN scheme (which the login UI enforces via /^\d{4}$/) is invalid and that an 8-char password is required — which the browser login form would then refuse to submit.
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. git diff shows app/passwords.py:48 changed `len(pw) < 8` to `len(pw) < 4` while line 49 still prints "password must be at least 8 characters". Reachable via the documented real workflow (`python -m app.passwords <user>`, per CLAUDE.md and the module docstring): a 3-char PIN entry triggers the branch and prints the stale message. The scenario's supporting detail is also accurate — webapp/js/app.js:1934 enforces /^\d{4}$/ ("4 xonali PIN kiriting"), so following the message's 8-character advice yields a credential the browser login form refuses to submit. Minor severity (misleading CLI message; enforcement itself is correct), but the defect is real and in the changed code.

confirmed.summary: Reys/adjust edit mode has no cancel path, so an abandoned edit silently turns the next save into a PUT that overwrites the old entry instead of creating a new one
confirmed.scenario: Kebab > Tahrirlash on a saved reys entry calls startEditReys (line 1032), setting editingReys. The only statements that clear editingReys/editingAdj are a successful save (lines 1747/1560), deleting that same entry (lines 1085-1086), and clearEntries on report open (lines 874-875). Telegram Back, showMenu/openWork navigation, and tab switches never clear them. A user who starts an edit, backs out to the menu, and later returns to record a genuinely new trip fills the form and hits save; onSave (line 1736) sees editingReys and PUTs /api/report/{id}, destroying the original entry's data. The shared Obshiy form clears editingEntry in formBack() (line 864) - the two backend-wired tabs have no equivalent, and there is literally no way to create a new entry in that tab while an edit is pending.
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. startEditReys (app.js:1034) / startEditAdjust (1051) set editingReys/editingAdj with no cancel path: onBack from the work area (361) goes to showMenu() which only swaps screens; openWork, setTab, and tab clicks never clear the vars; the only clearers are a successful save (1757/1570), deleting that same entry (1087-1088), and clearEntries via openReport (876-877, which also wipes all session entries). There is no cancel button, and formBack (866) covers only the shared Obshiy form. So an abandoned edit persists across navigation, and the next save in that tab — intended as a new trip — hits onSave's editing branch (1744-1747) and PUTs /api/report/{id}, where edit_reys reverses the old net and Object.assign overwrites the old entry client-side: the original entry's data and inventory contribution are replaced rather than a new entry added. Sole mitigation is the "Yangilash" button label (cosmetic, no functional guard); there is no way to create a new reys/adjust entry while the edit is pending. All this state machinery is new in the diff and reachable through real handlers (Telegram BackButton, menu buttons). Claim's line numbers drift ~2 lines but every mechanism checks out.

confirmed.summary: deleteEntry re-reads viewerSection after the awaited DELETE, so switching viewers while the request is in flight leaves the server-deleted entry in the local list
confirmed.scenario: deleteEntry (line 1063) checks viewerSection before the fetch, but `const list = state.entries[viewerSection]` (line 1080) runs after `await fetch(...)`. Over the cloudflared tunnel a DELETE can take seconds; if the user confirms deletion in the reys viewer, presses Back, and opens the adjust viewer (adjViewBtn) before the response lands, viewerSection is now 'adjust', `list.indexOf(entry)` is -1, and the function returns at line 1082 without splicing. The row is gone on the server (balances already updated at line 1072), but the reys list and its 'Yuklanganlar' badge still show the entry; tapping edit or delete on it then fails with 404 'yozuv topilmadi'.
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED. deleteEntry (webapp/js/app.js:1088, cited as 1063 before file drift) reads the shared mutable `viewerSection` both before and after the awaited DELETE fetch, with no snapshot and no busy-state guard. The entryDeleteBtn handler (line 1198) fires deleteEntry without awaiting, and during the in-flight request the viewer back button, work-area tabs, and the new reysViewBtn/adjViewBtn/topViewBtn "Yuklanganlar" buttons all remain clickable — any openEntries() call reassigns viewerSection. When that happens, `state.entries[viewerSection].indexOf(entry)` is -1 and deleteEntry returns early (line 1107), skipping the splice/updateViewCount/renderEntries. The server row is already deleted and balances already applied (line 1097), leaving a ghost entry in the reys list and badge; retrying delete or edit on it hits 404 "yozuv topilmadi" (server.py:612), and since the 404 throws before the splice, the ghost is unremovable for the rest of the session (until clearEntries on report reopen). The same diff snapshots `editingReys`/`editingAdj` before awaits in onSave/onAdjustSave, so the missing `const sec = viewerSection` snapshot here is a genuine oversight. Only nit: the reviewer's line numbers are ~23 lines stale and "balances updated at line 1072" is approximate, but the defect, navigation path, and consequences are all accurate.

confirmed.summary: Disabled lightbox nav arrows use pointer-events:none, so taps at the arrow position fall through to the backdrop and close the viewer
confirmed.scenario: `.lightbox__nav[disabled] { pointer-events: none }` (styles.css:706) combined with the backdrop close handler `if (e.target === els.lightbox) closeLightbox()` (app.js:664). User pages a multi-photo entry by repeatedly tapping the Next arrow; on the last photo lbShow disables it (app.js:600). The very next tap in the same spot targets whatever is underneath: wherever the letterboxed image does not extend under the arrow (portrait photos on desktop/wide viewports, or the 8-16px container padding strip the arrow overlaps at left/right:8px), the target is the .lightbox container itself, so the viewer abruptly closes mid-browsing instead of the arrow being an inert disabled button. Same for Prev at index 0.
confirmed.verdict: REAL. All claimed code exists in the diff: `.lightbox__nav[disabled] { opacity:0.25; pointer-events:none }` (styles.css:715), lbShow() disabling Prev/Next at the ends (app.js:609-610), and the backdrop close handler `if (e.target === els.lightbox) closeLightbox()` (app.js:689). Mechanics confirmed: a disabled button is normally inert (no click dispatched at all), but pointer-events:none removes it from hit-testing, so a tap at the arrow position targets the element underneath. The arrows sit at left/right:8px while .lightbox has 16px padding and the centered image (max-width/height:100%) never enters the padding — so the arrow's outer 8px strip always overlays the bare .lightbox container, and on wide/desktop viewports with portrait (letterboxed) photos the entire arrow footprint does. Clicks there make e.target === els.lightbox → closeLightbox(). No guard exists: touch handlers are passive (no preventDefault), no capture handler intercepts. Reachable through real navigation: entry thumbnails → viewEntryPhotos (app.js:986) or form thumbnails → openLightbox (app.js:403) with >1 photo; user pages via the Next arrow, reaches the last photo (arrow dims in place), and the next click/tap in the same spot closes the viewer instead of being inert — certain on desktop browser sessions (a first-class auth path per CLAUDE.md) and on the 8px padding strip on mobile. Only nuance: on phones with full-width photos, taps at the arrow's center land on the img and do nothing, so mobile impact is partial — but the claim scoped itself to exactly the letterboxed/padding cases. Minor-severity UX defect, but genuine and reachable.

summary: Adversarially review the entry backend-CRUD + Telegram-lightbox diff

workflowProgress.label: review:backend-consistency
workflowProgress.promptPreview:
Repo: C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/mandarin_reys_hisobot — Telegram Mini App: FastAPI backend (app/db.py, app/server.py) + vanilla-JS frontend (webapp/js/app.js, webapp/index.html, webapp/css/styles.css). Run "git diff" to see the change under review.

The diff adds:
BACKEND
- db.py: add_reys/adjust now return entry_id (lastrowid). New: _get_entry, _apply_balances (all-or-nothing per-type deltas, raises…
workflowProgress.resultPreview: {"findings":[{"summary":"The oversized-body rejection middleware `_limit_body` only checks `request.method == \"POST\"`, so the new multipart endpoints PUT /api/report/{entry_id} and PUT /api/adjust/{entry_id} completely bypass the MAX_BODY_BYTES (62 MB) cap. FastAPI resolves the Form/UploadFile parameters by parsing the entire multipart body (spooling file parts to temp files) BEFORE the handler …

workflowProgress.label: review:frontend-state
workflowProgress.promptPreview:
Repo: C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/mandarin_reys_hisobot — Telegram Mini App: FastAPI backend (app/db.py, app/server.py) + vanilla-JS frontend (webapp/js/app.js, webapp/index.html, webapp/css/styles.css). Run "git diff" to see the change under review.

The diff adds:
BACKEND
- db.py: add_reys/adjust now return entry_id (lastrowid). New: _get_entry, _apply_balances (all-or-nothing per-type deltas, raises…
workflowProgress.resultPreview: {"findings":[{"summary":"onSave's success handler unconditionally clears editingReys and calls resetForm(), clobbering an edit session started while the request was in flight — can silently create a duplicate reys entry (double-counted inventory) or wipe/corrupt the newly loaded edit form.","file":"webapp/js/app.js","line":1747,"scenario":"User taps 'Yangilash' to PUT entry X (all photos are re-up…
